frameworks.name: time-varying generalized linear model
frameworks.one_line: flexible psychophysical weights that drift trial-to-trial to capture strategy dynamics

frameworks.name: psychophysical weighting analysis
frameworks.one_line: standard sensitivity-to-stimulus model extended to include history covariates over time

frameworks.name: sensory decision-making
frameworks.one_line: perceptual choice tasks in mice, rats, and humans across modalities

core_question: How can we characterize trial-to-trial changes in an animal's decision-making strategy during learning and in trained subjects?
key_assumptions: decision strategies evolve smoothly over trials
choices depend on task-relevant stimuli and task-irrelevant history covariates
shared dynamics may exist across subjects within a population
manipulations: task-relevant stimulus features versus task-irrelevant covariates (choice, reward, stimulus history)
key_findings: method captures trial-to-trial changes in sensitivity to task stimuli
also tracks sensitivity to task-irrelevant covariates like choice, reward, and stimulus history
reveals adaptation to time-varying task statistics and suppression of sub-optimal strategies during learning
uncovers shared behavioral dynamics across subjects within a population
interpretations: standard static analyses miss systematic, time-varying components of behavior
decision strategies remain dynamic even in well-trained animals
